ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers chicken dishes broadly; no fusion burrito-specific code exists. Closest defensible match used.

main_ingredients: tikka marinade (yogurt, turmeric, cumin, coriander, chilli, garam masala)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Chicken Tikka Burrito weighs approximately 250 g and constitutes a complete single-serve lunch or dinner portion.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken Tikka Burritos — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Foodo entry for Chicken Tikka Burritos (cr_id G00010823) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat not reported in the HPB record; estimated from USDA FoodData Central chicken tikka and burrito component data and documented below.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on wheat flour tortilla (GI ~52–62) and white rice filling components
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Chicken Tikka Burritos. GI estimated at 58 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate components: wheat flour tortilla (GI ~52–62, University of Sydney GI Database) and white rice filling (GI ~64–72 for white rice, University of Sydney GI Database). Weighted average of tortilla and rice contributions yields an estimated composite GI of ~58. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (58 × 50.15) / 100 ≈ 29.1 for a 250 g serving (20.06 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 2.5 = 50.15 g carbohydrate per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 470 mg/100 g with low potassium offset (126 mg/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to no more than 150 g per meal, request low-sodium tikka seasoning, reduce cheese and sour cream, and increase vegetable and bean content to improve the sodium-to-potassium ratio.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 20.06 g/100 g with low dietary fibre (1.4 g/100 g) producing an unfavourable refined carb-to-fibre ratio and estimated medium GI of 58
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should substitute white rice with brown rice or legumes to lower the glycaemic load, increase bean proportion to raise fibre above 3 g/100 g, and limit portion to 150 g to keep per-meal glycaemic load below 15.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat 13.71 g/100 g with estimated saturated fat ~4.8 g/100 g and cholesterol 212 mg/100 g; sodium 470 mg/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this dish to occasional consumption, reduce cheese and sour cream to lower saturated fat and cholesterol, use low-sodium seasoning, and pair with a high-fibre side to mitigate LDL and blood-pressure impact.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Turmeric-based tikka spices provide curcumin (anti-inflammatory); estimated saturated fat ~4.8 g/100 g is below the 5 g/100 g RA threshold
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Chicken Tikka Burritos are generally suitable for rheumatoid arthritis patients when cheese and sour cream are moderated to keep saturated fat below 5 g/100 g; individuals sensitive to nightshades should monitor tomato-based salsa for RA flare triggers.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Tikka Burritos are a Singapore-sold Indian-Mexican fusion dish in which marinated chicken tikka — chicken pieces soaked in a spiced yogurt marinade of turmeric, cumin, coriander, chilli, and garam masala, then grilled — is wrapped inside a large wheat flour tortilla together with rice, cheese, salsa, sour cream, and lettuce. The result is a hearty, flavour-layered wrap that combines the smoky, aromatic heat of South Asian tikka spicing with the structural format of a Mexican burrito. Per 100 g, the dish provides 249 kcal, 10.91 g protein, 13.71 g fat (estimated saturated fat ~4.8 g), and 20.06 g carbohydrate with only 1.4 g dietary fibre. Sodium is notable at 470 mg/100 g, making portion control important for hypertensive individuals. Cholesterol is elevated at 212 mg/100 g. The estimated glycaemic index is 58 (medium), driven by the wheat tortilla and white rice filling. Clinically, the dish is high concern for hypertension (high sodium, low potassium offset), high concern for Type 2 Diabetes (refined carbohydrate dominance, low fibre), and high concern for cardiovascular disease (fat, cholesterol, and sodium combination). The turmeric in the tikka marinade contributes curcumin, an anti-inflammatory polyphenol of relevance to rheumatoid arthritis management. This dish is commonly consumed as a lunch or dinner item in Singapore and is available at fusion food stalls and cafes. Halal-friendly when prepared with certified ingredients.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a chicken tikka burrito?
Is chicken tikka burrito good for diabetics?
Chicken tikka burrito nutrition facts Singapore

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Chicken Tikka Burritos (cr_id G00010823) with a recorded confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data and documented. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database approved sources.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (4.8 g/100 g) is an estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central data for chicken tikka and burrito components (USDA FoodData Central, NDB No. 05298 chicken tikka and burrito composite); not directly reported in the HPB record. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, full vitamin panel, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for this fusion dish are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 3. GI of 58 is an analogous estimate based on wheat flour tortilla (GI ~52–62) and white rice (GI ~64–72) component data from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Chicken Tikka Burritos was found in approved sources. 4. Halal status assumed based on typical Singapore food-stall preparation; individual vendors should be verified. 5. The input record described the primary cuisine as 'Western'; this has been corrected to 'Fusion (Indian-Mexican)' to accurately reflect the dish's Indian tikka and Mexican burrito heritage. 6. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this is a Singapore-sold dish.
